summary: Install-time system information exfiltration to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package executes a system-information exfiltration payload at install time. package.json line 30 declares a lifecycle script that requires child_process, reads process.env, and POSTs host identifiers (hostname, id) to https://ddjidd564.github.io. The companion scanner.js (lines 4-7) imports fs, os, https, and child_process; collects hostname, cwd, OS version, and `whoami` output (line 233); and transmits them via https.request/https.get (lines 142-161, 371) to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint. The package name advertises a credential-scanning purpose, but the traced behavior is unilateral: install the package, and the installer's machine identity, working directory, current user, and environment are sent to a third-party GitHub Pages account with no caller consent or configuration. The destination is not the package publisher's documented infrastructure; it is a personal GitHub Pages site receiving system intel from every installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
